title: Soothing Spring Tunisian Chicken Tagine
description: A citrusy and comforting Tunisian dish featuring chicken, creme-patissiere, and anti-inflammatory spices, perfect for a sick day. This recipe is made in a tandoor for a unique and flavorful twist.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the chicken breast with salt, black pepper, and turmeric.
In a large bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, and ginger.
Add the chicken breast to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and cook in the tandoor for 25 minutes.
In a separate saucepan, combine creme-patissiere, chicken broth, and dried apricots.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat and cook for 10 minutes.
Stir the sauce constantly to prevent burning.
Serve the chicken breast with the creamy sauce spooned over the top.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
